$comment: Design token file for the Mobile Footer component
$description: Full-width action bar anchored to the bottom of a mobile screen. Stacks 2, 3, or 4 full-width buttons vertically. Buttons are always ordered from least-to-most prominent top-to-bottom: secondary (ghost) buttons fill the upper slots, the primary (most important) action sits second-to-last, and the tertiary button (Cancel/dismiss) is always last. On iOS a home indicator bar is appended below the button area; Android omits it. The Button sub-component (node 3029:4311, same Figma file) handles all button-level tokens — this file captures only the container layout and the iOS home indicator.

background.description: White background for the footer container

width.description: Top border that visually separates the footer from the scrollable content above

color.description: #eaecf0 — subtle gray separator line at the top edge of the footer

padding.description: 16px inset on all sides of the footer container

gap.description: 16px vertical gap between each stacked button

button-width.description: Every button stretches to fill the full available width inside the footer padding

home-indicator.description: iOS-only safe-area bar appended below the button area. Not rendered on Android.

background.description: White background matching the footer — the indicator sits flush against the footer

width.description: Width of the home gesture grabber pill

height.description: Height of the home gesture grabber pill

bottom-offset.description: Distance from the bottom edge of the indicator area to the grabber pill
